notes: Monthly chart redesigned with Week / Month / Year period selector
Week view honors the configured first day of week (Mon–Sun or Sun–Sat)
Month view spans the 1st to the last day of the current month
Year view buckets the current calendar year (Jan 1 – Dec 31) by week
New Expected hours line overlaid per X-axis bucket — accounts for workdays, holidays and personal vacation days
Info icon on the chart header shows a legend on hover
Toggle in Settings → Workday to show or hide the Expected line
Chart period selection (Week / Month / Year) is persisted across reloads
Thin gray vertical marker shows the bucket of the first recorded entry when visible in the current view (toggles together with the Expected line)
Intervals list integrated into the dashboard — no full-page navigation
Left column now switches between Calendar and Intervals via tabs
Tab selector lives in the top-left nav block, aligned with the panel below
Compact intervals layout with inline edit/delete and CSV export
Removed the standalone /intervals route
Holiday calendar distinguishes personal vacation days (indigo) from public holidays (amber)
Both types are discounted from hour counters; only personal days count toward the yearly quota
Type toggle in the calendar header chooses what gets added on click
Load preset dropdown imports a city's public holidays for a year (Barcelona 2026 included)
City/year presets are editable in src/app/data/public-holidays.ts
Unified Settings modal opened from the nav gear button
Sidebar tabs: Work schedule, Tracking, Focus, Data
Configurable workday: target hours/day, weekly target, workdays, first day of week, time format
Auto-round, lunch break and Pomodoro settings centralized in the modal
Save / Cancel flow: changes are buffered and only applied on Save
Data tab: export, import and reset — applied live without reloading the page
Reminder keeps running in the background via a dedicated service
